title: Columnea ochroleuca
intro: Columnea ochroleuca, also known as the Goldfish Plant or White and Gold Columnea, is a flowering plant species native to Central and South America, characterized by its bright white and yellow flowers. The health benefits of Columnea ochroleuca include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a promising candidate for treating various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Columnea ochroleuca include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, from gastrointestinal problems to respiratory infections.

As a member of the Gesneriaceae family, Columnea ochroleuca is a type of epiphytic or terrestrial plant, depending on the specific growing conditions.
